hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: Both halves of the score trail the Top 100 sample: HPS (818) sits well under the median (1226), and overheal (53.1%) lands between the sample's median (47.1%) and its single worst parse (58%) — closer to the weak end, though not the outright worst. Active time (99.5%) is essentially in line with the sample and isn't the story here. The more unusual number is 10 total raid deaths on this kill — a high count for a single Hydross pull — which the target-distribution section below connects to a real, checkable pattern in how narrowly this kill's healing was spread.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Union of both spell lists: Danceswtrees's 6 cast spells plus 2 benchmark-only Regrowth guids never cast (9857/9858, 0.9%/0.7% of the Top 100 average — shown honestly as 0% rather than omitted). The real gap is a HoT-mix shift toward Lifebloom: 58.5% vs. the sample's 41.4% (+17.1), while Regrowth (guid 26980) is underweighted at 19.5% vs. 27.6% (-8.1). Healing Touch is also modestly overweighted (3.3% vs. 1.4%).
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were both self-cast, in line with the sample's own majority pattern (64% and 100% self respectively) — not a deviation. Both Swiftmends went to Tauntt, matching the sample's near-universal "give it away" pattern (99% not-self). The real finding is Rebirth: Zamnes died at the 1,648.2s mark and was battle-rezzed 34.5 seconds later (1,682.7s) — a real, verified death-to-cooldown correlation, not the routine case (only 12% of the Top 100 sample used Rebirth at all here). Tree of Life uptime read 0% this kill — Danceswtrees does use the form on other kills this same raid night (see the raid overview), so this reflects a tactical choice not to shift here rather than a build limitation, but it is a real gap against the sample's 45.9% average. HPM (4.63) sits well below the Top 100 average (5.84), consistent with the scorecard's overheal shortfall — a meaningful share of the 41,123 mana spent this kill didn't convert to effective healing.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Only 3 distinct people received any healing this kill at all — every point of it, 100% coverage vs. the sample's own 83.7% (which itself spreads across more targets). Top-target concentration is the sharper gap: 72.7% of all healing went to Tauntt alone, vs. 48% for the Top 100 average (+24.7 points) — a genuinely tank-narrow healing pattern, not noise. This lines up with the scorecard's unusually high death count (10) for this kill: healing this concentrated on one target leaves little real capacity to cover raid members taking damage elsewhere, though the fight logs alone can't prove the deaths were caused by it rather than raid-wide mechanics.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (855) trails the Top 100 median (1531) by nearly half, but the sharper finding is overheal: 53.3% exceeds every parse in the Top 100 sample, including its single worst (49.1%) — this kill's overheal isn't just below average, it's outside the observed range entirely. Active time (97.8%) is close to the sample's average (98.4%), a minor gap next to the overheal shortfall. Despite the weak HPS/overheal combination, the 63rd percentile ranking reflects the sample's own spread on this boss more than a strong showing here — percentile and raw output tell two different parts of the story on this kill.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Different shape from Hydross: here BOTH Lifebloom (41.4% vs. 28.7%, +12.7) and Regrowth (47.6% vs. 38.7%, +8.9) run hot against the benchmark, at the expense of Rejuvenation (6.0% vs. 14.8%, -8.8) and Lifebloom's bloom-burst (3.4% vs. 8.8%, -5.4) — a HoT-refresh pattern that favors the two heavier spells over the cheaper filler ones, not a simple one-spell-for-another swap. Union of both lists adds two benchmark-only rows never cast (Regrowth guid 9858 at 2.2%, Healing Touch at 1.0%), shown honestly at 0% rather than omitted. Master Healthstone (0.2%, a warlock-provided consumable) doesn't appear in the Top 100 average at all — negligible either way.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused this kill despite 72% of the Top 100 sample using it — a real, notable absence, not routine. Innervate stayed self-cast, matching the sample's own majority (63% self). Two raid deaths occurred this kill (Dylfarian, Dodohpih); no Rebirth was attempted, but that's consistent with the sample's own norm (only 15% use it on this boss) rather than a gap specific to this kill — no meaningful cooldown-to-death correlation to draw here. Tree of Life again read 0% (see the raid overview for the pattern across kills). HPM (5.1) sits below the Top 100 average (7.34), tracking the overheal shortfall noted in the scorecard.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (69.8% vs. the sample's 56.1%, +13.7) and top-target concentration (43.4% vs. 24.6%, +18.8) both run well past the ±5 range that's shown to be normal variance — this kill's healing was genuinely more tank-focused than the Top 100 average, even with 20 distinct targets touched overall (a long thin tail, not a narrow one like Hydross). Self-healing (7.0% to Danceswtrees) is a real, visible share of the total — plausible for a Restoration Druid maintaining its own HoTs, not flagged as an issue on its own.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (773) sits at roughly half the Top 100 median (1468), and overheal (63.3%) exceeds every parse in the sample, including its single worst (54.3%) — the weakest scorecard combination of any kill this raid night by that measure. Active time (99.9%) is essentially at the top of the sample and isn't part of the story. 8 raid deaths on this kill — the most of any boss this raid night — is addressed directly in the cooldowns section below.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The largest spell-mix inversion of the whole raid night: Lifebloom (guid 33763) is overweighted at 66.4% vs. the sample's 19.1% (+47.3), while Regrowth is underweighted at 13.0% vs. 51.9% (-38.9) — the Top 100 approach to this boss specifically is Regrowth-dominant, and Danceswtrees's real cast mix runs almost the opposite way. (The benchmark's guid-33763 row is recorded under a French client's localized name, "Fleur de vie" — confirmed the same spell via guid, not display name, per the guid-grouping rule; shown here under its English name for consistency.) Union of both lists adds three benchmark-only spells never cast (Regrowth guid 9858 at 3.1%, Regrowth guid 9857 at 0.6%, Tranquility at 0.5%), shown honestly at 0%. Master Healthstone (0.9%) doesn't appear in the Top 100 average at all.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was never cast despite 8 raid deaths this kill — the most of any boss tonight — and 25% of the Top 100 sample does use one here, a real if modest gap worth naming (the fight logs can't say whether any specific one of these 8 deaths was actually rez-safe to attempt). Checking the deaths against cooldown timestamps directly: a Swiftmend landed on Tauntt within 1 second of Unstraight's death, and a self-cast Swiftmend followed roughly 8 seconds before Olmar's death — both are coincidental timing rather than a targeted save, since neither Swiftmend's target matches the player who died. Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were both self-cast, in line with the sample's own majority pattern. Tree of Life again read 0% this kill. HPM (5.02) trails the Top 100 average (6.39), consistent with the overheal gap.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (86.5% vs. the sample's 51.1%, +35.4) is a large gap — despite 15 distinct targets receiving some healing, nearly all of it landed on just these top 5. Top-target concentration (29.9% vs. 15.6%, +14.3) is real but less extreme than the coverage gap. Together they describe a kill where healing was spread thin across a long tail of targets in name only — the effective spread was much narrower than the Top 100 average, which likely connects to the 8 deaths noted in the scorecard, though that link isn't provable from healing data alone.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1314) trails the Top 100 median (1796) by a meaningful margin, and overheal (49.3%) exceeds every parse in the sample, including its single worst (46.5%). Active time (99.9%) is at the top of the sample and isn't part of the story. 5 raid deaths landed in a tight ~77-second window mid-kill — the cooldowns section below checks this against what was and wasn't cast in that window.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The second kill in a row with the same extreme inversion seen on Leotheras: Lifebloom is overweighted at 84.5% vs. the sample's 38.4% (+46.1), while Regrowth is nearly absent at 3.6% vs. 34.1% (-30.5). Swiftmend, cast by 92% of the Top 100 sample here, wasn't cast at all this kill — a real, sizable absence, not just a rounding gap. Union of both lists adds three more benchmark-only rows never cast (Healing Touch 1.2%, Regrowth guid 9856 1.2%, Rejuvenation guid 26981 0.8%), all shown honestly at 0%.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: The 5 raid deaths this kill (Sixdegrees ×2, Appletini, Shoktopuss, Hardtag) all landed inside a tight ~77-second window — and across that whole window, neither Nature's Swiftness (used by 79% of the sample) nor Swiftmend (used by 92%) was cast even once. Both are real, checkable absences during the exact stretch the raid was dying, though the logs can't confirm either would have prevented a specific death. The single Innervate this kill was self-cast about 5 seconds after the last of the 5 deaths — routine end-of-window timing, not connected to any of them. HPM (7.85) actually lands almost exactly on the Top 100 median (7.88) despite the overheal shortfall — mana efficiency here is closer to typical than the rest of this kill's numbers suggest.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (91.6% vs. the sample's 73.2%, +18.4) and top-target concentration (45% vs. 33.3%, +11.7) both run past the ±5 normal-variance range — a real, tank-narrower spread than the Top 100 average, consistent with the pattern seen on most other kills this raid night.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: The most balanced scorecard of the raid night so far: HPS (1163) still trails the Top 100 median (1413), but overheal (41.8%) sits close to the sample's own median (39.8%) and stays well inside the observed range — a real improvement over Hydross through Karathress, where overheal ran past even the sample's worst parse. Active time (99.9%) is again near the top of the sample.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Same overweighted-Lifebloom / underweighted-Regrowth shape seen on Leotheras and Karathress, though noticeably smaller here: 60.9% vs. 36.7% (+24.2) and 11.6% vs. 29.6% (-18.0). Union of both lists adds two benchmark-only rows never cast (Regrowth guid 9858 at 1.0%, Tranquility at 1.2%), shown honestly at 0%.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: The clearest death-to-cooldown correlation of the raid night: Érel died at the 6,197.8s mark and was battle-rezzed just 3.9 seconds later — faster than the equivalent save on Hydross. Both Swiftmends went to Zamnes, matching the sample's near-universal "give it away" pattern (94% not-self). Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were both self-cast, matching the sample's own majority. HPM (6.85) is the closest to its Top 100 average of any kill this raid night (avg 7.08, a gap of only -0.23) — the strongest mana-efficiency showing so far.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: A genuine outlier for this raid night: coverage (67.5% vs. 67.3%, +0.2) and top-target concentration (38.7% vs. 38.4%, +0.3) both land within a fraction of a point of the Top 100 average — the closest alignment of any kill this raid night. Every other kill so far has run several points narrower/more tank-concentrated than the sample — this is the one kill where Danceswtrees's real target spread lines up with the norm almost exactly, across 22 distinct targets touched overall.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: Overheal (45.9%) lands almost exactly on the Top 100 median (44.8%) — the closest-to-typical overheal result of the raid night. The real outlier is active time: 95.8%, well below the sample's average (99.2%) and median (99.7%), a genuine gap rather than noise. This kill's own death list explains it directly — Danceswtrees died at the 7,559.6s mark, inside a dense end-of-fight cluster of 6 deaths within 12 seconds (see target distribution below) — the reduced active time is real downtime from that death, not idle behavior earlier in the pull.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: A much smaller gap than the last three kills: Lifebloom (54.5% vs. 46.3%, +8.2) and Regrowth (27.7% vs. 21.3%, +6.4) are both only modestly overweighted, at the expense of Rejuvenation (6.9% vs. 11.9%, -5.0) and the Lifebloom bloom-burst specifically (5.5% vs. 12.4%, -6.9 — the largest single gap on this kill). Swiftmend and Healing Touch both track the benchmark closely. Two benchmark-only rows (Regrowth guid 9858 at 1.2%, Rejuvenation guid 26981 at 0.5%) were never cast, shown at 0%.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Largebingus died once at the 7,261.4s mark, was battle-rezzed 154.6 seconds later (7,416.1s), then died a second time at 7,570.7s inside the fight's final death cluster — a real, traceable death → rez → second death sequence, not just a routine cooldown use. Swiftmend was cast 7 times, right in line with the Top 100 average (7), all given away rather than self-cast, matching the sample's 95% not-self norm. Tree of Life uptime (93.7%) is a real standout — well above the Top 100 average (65.7%) on this specific kill, on this fight's long duration a sustained Tree form clearly paid off. HPM (6.19) trails the average modestly (6.88), a smaller gap than most of tonight's other kills.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration is the largest gap of the raid night: 44.9% to Zamnes alone vs. the sample's 21.2% (+23.7, more than double). Coverage (66.6% vs. 50.9%, +15.7) is also well past normal variance. The fight's final seconds saw a dense death cluster spanning just 12 seconds (Danceswtrees, Sixdegrees, Grobaladin, Largebingus, Zamnes, Wubblez, in addition to Nicole and Jonnypork earlier) — consistent with a long, grinding kill that came down to the wire rather than a clean finish.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (831) sits well under the Top 100 median (1050), and overheal (56.3%) is above the sample's median (50.2%) though still inside the observed range (worst 61.1%). Active time (99.2%) matches the Top 100 average exactly — a real, if unremarkable, point of consistency.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The familiar shape continues: Lifebloom overweighted (70.7% vs. 49.9%, +20.8) against an underweighted Regrowth (12.3% vs. 20.9%, -8.6). Swiftmend is also notably underweighted here (1.9% vs. 5.8%, -3.9), which lines up with the cooldown table below showing far fewer Swiftmend casts than typical. One benchmark-only row (Rejuvenation guid 26981, 1.3%) was never cast, shown at 0%.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Zamnes died at the 9,625.7s mark and was battle-rezzed just 3.8 seconds later — the fastest of the three real death-to-Rebirth saves confirmed this raid night (Hydross 34.5s, Morogrim 3.9s, this one 3.8s). The bigger real gap is Swiftmend: only 2 casts vs. a Top 100 average of 6.2 — a genuine shortfall in reactive healing use, not just a rounding difference, and it lines up with the underweighted Swiftmend share noted in spell composition. Innervate and Nature's Swiftness both stayed self-cast, matching the sample's majority.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (93.2% vs. 78.6%, +14.6) runs past normal variance — the highest coverage number of the raid night, meaning healing was almost entirely captured by just these 5 people out of 15 distinct targets touched. Top-target concentration (46.2% vs. 43.1%, +3.1) is a smaller, within-normal-range gap this time — closer to typical than most other kills tonight.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: This boss's Top 100 sample runs a much tighter overheal range than any other kill tonight (best 10.4%, worst 33.2%) — Danceswtrees's 39.4% sits outside that entire range, a real and specific gap on this particular fight, not just "below average" the way most other kills read. HPS (1385) is well under the sample median (2408), consistent with the wider overheal gap. Active time (99.3%) is close to the sample average and isn't the story here.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The most inverted matchup of the entire raid night: the Top 100 sample treats Void Reaver as an almost pure Regrowth fight (75.6% of all healing), with Lifebloom barely used (4.7%) — Danceswtrees runs the opposite mix, Lifebloom-heavy (70.5%) with Regrowth a distant second (20.6%, -55.0 against the benchmark). This is the same Lifebloom-leaning style used on every other boss tonight, applied here without adapting to what the sample shows is actually the efficient approach for this specific fight. Union of both lists adds four benchmark-only rows never cast (Regrowth guids 9858/9856/9857 at 5.2%/1.5%/1.2%, and Tranquility at 1.1%), all shown honestly at 0%.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: The one real positive standout of the raid night: HPM (8.91) actually exceeds both the Top 100 average (8.32) and median (8.29) — despite the overheal shortfall noted in the scorecard, mana was spent more efficiently here than on any other kill tonight. The 6 raid deaths this kill clustered within a ~108-second window; no Rebirth was cast, but that matches the sample's own norm (only 7% usage rate on this boss) rather than reading as a specific gap. Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were both self-cast, in line with the sample.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: The largest coverage gap of the raid night by far: 83.1% vs. the sample's own 37.3% (+45.8) — the Top 100 sample spreads healing very thin on this specific fight (consistent with its known room-wide damage pattern), while Danceswtrees's healing stayed concentrated in the usual handful of targets. Self-healing is unusually high here too (23.3% to Danceswtrees), by far the largest self-heal share of any kill tonight — a plausible, boss-appropriate response to raid-wide damage rather than a concern on its own.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: The highest overheal number of the entire raid night: 67.4%, exceeding even the Top 100 sample's single worst parse (64.7%). Active time (93.4%) is also the lowest of any kill tonight, well below the sample average (97.5%) — but unlike Vashj, neither of this kill's 2 deaths was Danceswtrees's own, so this gap isn't self-explained the same way; on a short 165s fight, any brief inactive stretch (plausibly tied to Solarian's known movement mechanic, though not confirmable from this data) counts for a larger share of total time than it would on a longer kill.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The one boss this raid night where Danceswtrees's real approach actually matches the Top 100 sample's own: both are Regrowth-dominant here (65.7% vs. 59.6%), the only kill where that's true rather than inverted. Rejuvenation is the clearer gap (1.6% vs. 9.7%, -8.1). One benchmark row ("癒合") is recorded in the Top 100 data without a guid suffix, unlike every other cross-referenced spell on this page — shown as-is rather than guessed at, per the guid-grouping rule; it wasn't cast by Danceswtrees this kill either way. Four more benchmark-only rows (Regrowth guid 9857 1.5%, Healing Touch 1.8%, Tranquility 0.6%) were never cast, shown honestly at 0%.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: This is the one boss tonight where the Top 100 sample's own norm for Innervate is to give it away (only 40% self-cast) — Danceswtrees kept it self-cast, the minority pattern here specifically, a real reversal from every other kill's self-cast-matches-majority result. The same reversal shows on Swiftmend: 90% of the sample gives it away, but Danceswtrees's single cast went to self. Nature's Swiftness went unused despite 66% of the sample using it — the third kill this raid night with that same gap (after Lurker and Karathress). Tree of Life uptime (100%) is the highest of the raid night, more than triple the sample average (33.2%) — full Tree form for this entire short kill. HPM (3.04) is the lowest of any kill tonight and well under the average (4.79), consistent with the scorecard's steep overheal number.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: The one boss this raid night where Danceswtrees is their own #1 recipient (33.9%, more than double the sample's typical top-target share of 16.2%) — a real, boss-specific pattern likely tied to Solarian's mechanics targeting individual raid members including healers, rather than a tank-focused fight like most of tonight's other kills. Coverage (66.4% vs. 52.5%, +13.9) is a smaller, more typical gap by comparison.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: consistent on the large majority of slots and gems, with two real, explained deviations: a one-kill mainhand/relic swap on The Lurker Below, and a boots enchant that appears partway through the raid (from Al'ar onward)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Ring enchants aren't flagged, per this era's self-only Enchanting restriction. The one real, unambiguous gap here is the cloak's missing permanent enchant, consistent across the entire raid night — a straightforward, no-tradeoff fix.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Kael'thas Sunstrider wasn't killed this raid night (9 of 10 SSC/TK bosses downed) — no page exists for it, correctly, rather than a placeholder. Four real patterns hold up across the 9 real kills: HPS trails the Top 100 average on every single kill, no exceptions. Overheal exceeds the Top 100 sample's single worst parse on 5 of 9 kills (Lurker, Leotheras, Karathress, Void Reaver, Solarian) — a real, recurring gap on more than half the night, not one-off bad luck. Lifebloom ran hotter than its own Top 100 benchmark share on all 9 kills, but the size of that gap varies enormously by boss — from a modest +7.5 points on Solarian and +8.2 on Vashj up to +65.8 on Void Reaver, +47.3 on Leotheras, and +46.1 on Karathress (three of the last four kills of the night). Void Reaver stands out specifically because the sample's own approach there is Regrowth-dominant (75.6%), not Lifebloom-led like every other boss — Danceswtrees's real cast mix doesn't adapt to that. Solarian is the one kill where Danceswtrees's own dominant spell (Regrowth, 65.7%) actually matches the sample's own dominant spell there (59.6%), even though Lifebloom is still individually overweighted. Tree of Life saw real, heavy use on only 2 of 9 kills (Vashj 93.7%, Solarian 100%) and read a flat 0% on the other 7 — a consistent, worth-investigating pattern rather than fight-by-fight noise. Three real, verified death-to-Rebirth correlations were found across the night (Hydross 34.5s, Morogrim 3.9s, Al'ar 3.8s after each respective death) — see each boss page's cooldowns section for the specifics.

GearCheckItems.Description: 13 non-meta gems (all Living Ruby) plus 1 meta gem in the head slot, confirmed identical on all 9 snapshots
Mainhand was a Fishing Pole instead of the real weapon, relic slot emptied, on The Lurker Below only
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Not a gearing issue — this is the same benign, deliberate swap for the Lurker Below pull specifically seen on this healer's other raid nights, confirmed real from the same combatantinfo snapshot used for every other check on this page.

GearCheckItems.Description: Boots gained a permanent enchant partway through the raid — absent on the first 6 kills, present from Al'ar onward
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real mid-raid gearing change, not an inconsistency to flag as a problem — the boots went from unenchanted to enchanted between Vashj and Al'ar.

GearCheckItems.Description: One relic-type item is quality 3 (rare), while every other equipped item is quality 4 (epic)
